title: Chapter 44: The First Seat Descends
content: The sky above Blackwood University did not tear.
It simply... stopped.
The violet light vanished. The clouds froze mid-drift. The wind died. Everything went still — not the stillness of peace, but the stillness of a held breath.
And then the First Seat descended.

It did not come through a portal or a rift. It came through the fabric of reality itself, stepping out of the seam between what was and what could be. Its form was no longer an invisible presence — it had taken shape.
It was a figure in black. Humanoid. Featureless. But behind it, the sky was filled with eyes. Thousands of them. All open. All watching.
"Roxanne Vance."
The First Seat's voice was not loud. It was everywhere.
"You have destroyed forty thousand years of order. You have collapsed the Exchange. You have broken the Council. You have restored a dead system."
Roxanne stood in the quad, surrounded by her allies. Silas. The Executioner. Frost. Kallos. Lena, sketchbook in hand.
"I replaced a broken system with a better one."
"You replaced order with chaos."
"I replaced debt with freedom. There's a difference."
The First Seat's form shifted. Its featureless face grew features — not a human face, but the shape of one. Old. Severe. Disappointed.
"You have no card now. No infinite wealth. No contract. You are just a mortal girl with nothing."
Roxanne looked at her empty hand.
"I have more than I ever had."
"What?"
"A world that doesn't need your permission."
She stepped forward.
"You can try to destroy me. But the Void is back. The contract is active. Every entity in the Nether-Grid knows it. You can't un-ring that bell."
The First Seat's eyes — all thousand of them in the sky — narrowed.
"Then I will destroy the Void again."
"You couldn't do it alone the first time. You needed allies. And you don't have them anymore."
Behind the First Seat, the sky flickered.
The Seventh Seat's constellation appeared. Then the Sixth Seat's smoke spiral. Then the Fifth Seat's stone form.
The Council had arrived.
"The First Seat is in violation of the supermajority rule," the Seventh Seat said. "This action was not authorized."
"The First Seat does not require authorization."
"The First Seat does now. The rules changed."
The First Seat's thousand eyes turned to face the Council.
"You would side with a mortal over me?"
"We would side with the contract over you," the Sixth Seat said. "The Void is restored. The original system is active. Your authority is void."
The First Seat's form flickered.
For the first time, Roxanne saw something in its posture that she had never seen before.
Fear.
